parameter_tuning.principle: AI may recommend formula parameters, but the formula definitions remain deterministic and auditable.

formulas.name: return_n
formulas.interpretation: Positive values indicate appreciation over the lookback.
formulas.limitations: Requires consistent adjusted or raw close convention.

formulas.name: ema
formulas.definition: EMA_t = alpha * value_t + (1 - alpha) * EMA_t_minus_1; alpha = 2 / (n + 1)

formulas.name: macd
formulas.definition: EMA(12) - EMA(26), signal = EMA(9) of MACD, histogram = MACD - signal
formulas.interpretation: MACD above signal supports bullish momentum.

formulas.name: rsi
formulas.interpretation: Above 70 often overbought; below 30 often oversold.

formulas.name: bollinger_bands
formulas.definition: middle = SMA(20), upper = middle + 2 * stdev, lower = middle - 2 * stdev
formulas.interpretation: Band width measures compression/expansion; percent B locates price inside or outside bands.

formulas.name: atr
formulas.definition: Wilder-smoothed average of true range, where true range is max(high-low, abs(high-prev_close), abs(low-prev_close))
formulas.interpretation: Higher ATR indicates larger recent price ranges.

formulas.name: stochastic_k
formulas.interpretation: Above 80 often overbought; below 20 often oversold.

formulas.name: obv
formulas.definition: Cumulative volume added on up closes and subtracted on down closes.
formulas.interpretation: Trend and divergence are more useful than absolute value.

formulas.name: mfi
formulas.interpretation: Above 80 indicates strong buying pressure/overbought; below 20 selling pressure/oversold.
